inline_comments.message:
I am not sure did we clearly agree scope with team for this user story?!
You seem to focus (just on) cm-handle names for now
also if anchor names are vlaidation maybe we need NOT to valid CM -handles one will follow from teh other...
I think any validation (of cm-handle names) should be done in a utility class that can be used anywhere NCMP
ddi you get any input from anyone/anywhere on this?

not suer about this part:
it more like FOR_CM_HANDLE_NAMES  (need to confirm when I see code)
we'll probably get a SonarQube security hotspot for this but we can address it being not user input
As suggested in Jira ticket, I think that it should be good to also include "-" (dash) as an allowed character for dataspace, schema set and anchor names as it is quite common.
The validation logic with the regex should be implemented at unique common place like an utility class.
is there no lower level unit test for this already?!
this is a util class, validation should not happen here but at point of entry ie. public api methods...
should validate BEFORE create the object with teh invalide parameter

no need for scenarios (test regex)
maybe test just with "cm handle with spaces"
not neede here, tested in exceptionHandleSpec isntead
There is, have removed this test as a result.

Have decided to take a route to exclude characters based on the implementation proposal

https://wiki.onap.org/pages/resumedraft.action?draftId=128714061&draftShareId=20fa0439-8f58-4182-9006-088638583d88&
Context of this test has also changed now too. based on code changes to verification of CM-Handle ID's
I can see some issue here lated to Renu's task. I think the best strategy is to merge your validation first and then renu can see how to use it best
inconsistent with message on line #91 I think the one on line #91 is better
I didn't think you wre using this?
you didn't change this file but copyright update is probably OK

nameOrIds

(maybe check all the calls and just IDs will sufices if so change constant and method name too, we can dicuss if needed)
see naming discussion above, I am starting to think that just id is probably best...

I think thre is betetr ways of dsoing this expcially using static and do it oncce
See also https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/iterate-over-the-characters-of-a-string-in-java/

I think I would define/use something like this 
private static char[] INVALID_ID_CHARACTERS = "...".toCharArray();
I thought we agreed to accept - so no need to change this
Yes, me and Renu discussed this yesterday. Renu said to put the validation here for now, and then she will work around it.
You're right. This was part of an older commit for the apache validator library.
I changed this file as part of a previous commit. Can revert, but you're right. I don't think it's necessary?
I am not sure if it can work that way. Because we have to iterate over the characters that are passed in as part of the id parameter to see if they match any of the characters in the string above.
name? (check what we call it in open API id or name)
no its OK we probably did change this file at some stage already :-)

In the rest input, it is just cm handle. But we consistently call this cm handle id throughout the code. It is never referred to as cm handle name. 

That's why I chose to leave this as ID. Can change to name if necessary?

I didn't put validation here, as dataspace isn't a user inputted parameter here.

This method belongs to the following api.
/v1/ch/searches

This queries cm handles based on module names. Which we didn't specify as part of the validation criteria in the initial user story.

I can add validation for module names too as part of this commit?
Dataspace and schemaset also aren't user inputted here. Validation is done at earlier stage as part of cm handle creation.

need a bit of update:
- introduce CpsValidotr
- applied to all relevant public Java APIs
- update tets where needed
it is a public Java API even if not used with user input now it could be at any time....
as before public interface should be validated in any case
as before public interface should be validated in any case
as before public interface should be validated in any case
NB: Copyright slightly out of alignment
NB: Could add to message: "the following characters are not supported for cm handles in CPS !“” #$%&‘()*+,./\\:;<=>?@[]^`{|}~"
NB: could use when/then and use string only once in the the when block
Have changed to 2021 as this is when it was last edited by a member of nordix foundation.
With this dependency we are coupling this utility class with service spi and we'll not be able to use in a different context. Are we ok with this ?

There is inconsistency here which me and Toine realized. But everywhere else within CPS we use ID for cm-handle and not name. We decided to reflect this in the message thrown as part of the Data validation exception in the utility class.

Data validation exception is already coupled within the utility package as part of the JsonObjectMapper utility class and the Yangutils utility. I presumed it would be okay to use it in the came context here?
proble reintroce constant String (From wich you can create the list of cahr still now you are using this in teh error message...
extract you UNSUPPORTED_NAME_CHRACTERS_AS_STRING so you can use it in error message below too

I kept this name as Characters as I feel it still makes sense?

If you think this should explicitly be called UNSUPPORTED_NAME_STRING I can change it.

NB: add space
(If that is all you change I can +2 again without waiting on others)
Validations are usually the first thing in a flow.
cm-handle has an invalid character(s) in id.

The intention of this test is check if correct objects is created using createFailureResponse method. It is not to test each Registration error.

If you want to do it, the where clause can-be used.
cmhandle with invalid character is not testing a new functionality in this class. Is it possible to removed or merged it with the previous test?

We have added the validation but we are not testing the flow if validation fails then nothing is processed. 

It would be a lot of testing but I am not sure if it is okay to ignore it.
NB: indexOf is 0 based. It would be easier for reader if it is 1-based indexed.
good to validate the index position too in the error message.
I initially added testing for all these cases, but thought just testing the CpsValidator itself would suffice as it would be the same kind of scenario.
I personally prefer starting from 0 because it starts from the beginning of the first letter. What does the rest of the team think of this?

I think I agree, we do need a simple test for each method that now uses the validator.
They are simple test and can be added in a few minutes...
depends on the target audience, we assumed developers but end-user is more likely and Renu's point is valid and we should add 1
Will add these as part of a separate commit on the same Jira ticket
NB: Any reason the unsupported character is not also provided in the message ?
